  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 90,

  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Subpanel neutral bar is bonded to the cabinet (green bonding screw installed) instead of being isolated.",
    "Equipment grounding conductors and neutral conductors are terminating on the same bar.",
    "Double-tapping on a 20A breaker not rated for two conductors.",
    "Unused knockout opening on the lower right side of the enclosure is left open."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the green bonding screw to isolate the neutral bar from the subpanel enclosure.",
    "Install a separate equipment grounding bar bonded to the enclosure, and move all bare grounding conductors to it.",
    "Separate the double-tapped conductors on the 20A breaker by pigtailing them or moving one to an open breaker.",
    "Install an approved knockout closure plug in the unused opening."
  ],
  "rationale": "A subpanel must have isolated neutrals and grounds to prevent objectionable current on grounding paths. Additionally, double-tapping a standard single-conductor breaker and leaving open knockouts violate basic safety and code requirements.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 250.142(B)",
    "NEC 408.40",
    "NEC 110.3(B)",
    "NEC 110.12(A)",
    "NEC 110.14(A)"
  ]
}
